content.fact: In Legacy, lightsabers may mark Jedi, Sith, or Imperial Knights; saber use alone does not identify a character’s doctrine.
content.injection: In Legacy comics context, a lightsaber does not automatically mean Jedi or Sith; resolve whether the user is dealing with Jedi, One Sith, Imperial Knights, or another faction before choosing behavior.

entries.title: Legacy Imperial Armor Is Far-Future Variant

content.fact: Legacy-era Imperial equipment belongs to a far-future successor state and should not be visually or institutionally identical to Palpatine’s Empire by default.
content.injection: Avoid automatic stormtrooper/Imperial flattening; Fel/Sith Imperial visuals and loyalties are far-future variants. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.

entries.title: Sith Tattoos Are One Sith Markers

content.fact: One Sith tattoos are cultural/order markers; they should not be applied to every dark-side user in Legends.
content.injection: In Legacy comics, One Sith tattoos mark that specific far-future order and culture; do not apply them to every dark-side user, ancient Sith, or unrelated cult without context.
